name: Eight Layer Taco Spread
directions: Stir together beans and picante or taco sauce.
Spread bean mixture about 1/4 inch thick, on a 12 inch platter, leaving a 2 inch open rim at edge of platter.
Then layer sour cream, guacamole, cheese, onion, olives and lettuce.
Cover and chill up to 24 hours.
Before serving, arrange tomatoes on top.
Arrange the chips on a platter around spread.

name: Tomatillo Salsa
directions: Combine ingredients.
Use as needed.

name: Jen's Guacamole
directions: Peel, pit and mash avocados, saving ONE pit.
Stir in sour cream. Continue to stir until somewhat smooth (will still have some small lumps).
Add cilantro.
Drain tomatoes, almost completely (keep a little liquid in with them).
Add and stir until mixed well. Place avocado pit in with dip (this will keep avocado from turning brown). Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour or until ready to serve.

name: Guacamole - the Right Way!
directions: Grind spices together with mortar & pestle.  Add to rest of ingredients, partially mashing the avocado – leaving at least ½ in good-sized chunks.  Cover tightly with plastic wrap and chill overnight.  Taste for seasonings, then sprinkle with a bit more paprika, some more fresh cilantro leaves and diced red onion on top, and serve with tortilla chips.

name: Chunky Guacamole
directions: Scrape out the avocado from skin
Smash w/ fork til you have a chunky paste
Add onions and mix well.
Add tomatos and salt.
Finally add the cilantro.
Serve immediately.

name: Simple Nacho Dip - Yummy!
directions: Spray glass baking dish (I use rectangular) with cooking spray for easier cleaning.
Place both packages of cream cheese in bottom of baking dish. Microwave x 1 minute to soften then spread to coat entire bottom of dish.
Sprinkle lightly with garlic powder.
Top with both cans of chili.
Sprinkle drained olives & spread evenly over chili.
Next sprinkle onion layer over olives.
Top with cheddar.
Cover with foil.
Bake 350 degrees x 20-25 minutes. Remove foil & bake another 10 minutes until cheese melted.

name: Jalapeno Spread/Dip
directions: add all together into a food processor; turn on for approximately 2 minutes to make sure everything is well mixed.
pour into a bowl and serve immediately or cover and chill in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
if it tastes too much like mayo, add more jalapenos and more jalapeno juice.
its up to you.

name: Traditional Guacamole
directions: Cut the avocados in half, then take out the pits.
With a spoon scrape the avocado into a small mixing bowl and mashed it up.
Add the Lime, Salt, Onions, Tomatoes and chopped green jalapeno peppers.
The salt and Lime go to preferred taste.

name: Firecracker Appetizer Pie
directions: Crust:.
Preheat oven to 325°F.
Combine crushed cereal and butter.  Mix well.
Press firmly into the bottom of 9-inch springform pan.
Bake 10 to 15 minutes until golden brown and firm.
Filling:.
Beat cream cheese and eggs.
Add green chilies, jalapenos and cheese. Stir well to combine.
Pour over baked crust.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes, until center is firm.
Allow to cool slightly.
Place on serving plate. Loosen sides of the pan with a knife and remove.
Spread top of pie with sour cream.
Garnish with black olives, green onions, tomatoes.
Serve with crusted baguette slices or tortilla chips.

name: Artichoke-Parmesan Crostini
directions: Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
Stir together first 6 ingredients.
Spread 1 tablespoon artichoke mixture on each baguette slice and place on ungreased baking sheets.
Bake for 6 to 7 minutes or until golden brown and bubbly.  Serve hot.

name: Taco Scoop
directions: Soften cream cheese.
Brown hamburger.
Mix in taco seasoning as directed.
Mix cream cheese and sour cream together. (Use more or less sour cream to get the consistency that you want.).
Mix some taco seasoning into cream cheese or use other seasoning to your discretion.
Layer ingredients in order on a large sheet pan or whatever works for you.
Let each person top theirs with salsa or other condiments.
Use tortilla chips to scoop.
Enjoy!

name: Poblano Queso Dip
directions: In a saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter. Add garlic and peppers; sauté until tender, about 2 minutes. Add white wine and bring to a simmer. Add the cheeses and stir until melted. Season to taste. Serve warm with Piller's Pepperoni Minis.
Refrigerate any leftover dip for up to 24 hours. Reheat and serve hot over baked potatoes or cooked pasta with sliced Piller's Pepperoni Minis.
More information and recipe ideas are available online at pillers.com.

name: Jalapeno Popper Dip With Panko
directions: Preheat oven to 375.
Mix cream cheese, mayonnaise, Mexican cheese blend, green chilies, jalapenos, bacon and 1/2 cup parmesan together and spread in a 2 quart baking dish.
Mix panko, remaining parmesan and butter until incorporated, and sprinkle over top.
Bake 20 - 25 minutes until top is lightly browned and bubbly.
